questions.prompt: How should execution gate at the risky architectural bets (T8 router, T11 headless, T12 stealth, T13 vision)?
questions.description: Each big swing has a defined spike + go/no-go in sequence.md §5. The question is whether /goal should HALT for your decision after each spike, or push through autonomously.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Gate each big swing behind its spike — run the spike, report findings, and STOP for my go/no-go before committing the implementation.

options.label: Gate big swings (spike → stop → my decision)
Stop at the end of every tier (A/B/C/D)
Run straight through, autonomous

questions.prompt: Is a reliability eval harness in scope?
questions.description: The report's whole reliability thesis is 'measure with n>=8 runs; single-run scores are noise.' T7 (planner/validator/drift) can't be proven without measurement. How far do we go?
questions.recommendedAnswer: Yes — a lightweight internal harness (a handful of repeatable task scenarios, n>=8 runs, pass-rate + token tracking) so T7's gains are measured, not eyeballed. Not a full public benchmark port.

options.label: Lightweight internal harness (n>=8, our own scenarios)
Full benchmark port (WebVoyager / Online-Mind2Web)
No harness — ship the technique, manual spot-check

questions.prompt: Which zero-friction default model path(s) should T5 ship? (select all acceptable)
questions.description: Goal: the product does something before the user connects a provider. Each path has tradeoffs — free-tier keys have ToS/quota exposure; OAuth-sub reuse needs flow upkeep; local models fail structured output for the agent loop.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Ship guided OAuth-subscription reuse (ChatGPT/Copilot/Gemini) AND a bundled free-tier key path; keep BYO as the power path; do NOT make local Ollama the default agent loop.

options.label: Guided OAuth-subscription reuse (no new credentials)
Bundled free-tier API key (Gemini Flash / NVIDIA)
Local-model option (Ollama) as a selectable path
Keep BYO only (don't ship a default path)

questions.prompt: What's the real multitask use case driving T8/T11?
questions.description: This sets how far the session router and headless runtime go. The T8 spike decides the physical ceiling regardless, but the target shape drives the design.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Both: a few concurrent interactive windows for daily driving AND many background/headless sessions for fleet-style automation. Design the registry kind-neutral so both are the same abstraction.

options.label: Both interactive windows + background fleet
Mainly many background/headless sessions (fleet)
Mainly a few concurrent interactive windows

questions.prompt: If the T12 spike shows the extension can't be hidden (chrome.debugger infobar is mandatory), what then?
questions.description: The independent benchmark says the control plane, not patches, decides survivability. The extension path may be inherently non-stealth; the headless/direct-CDP path (T11) can be.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Accept it: stealth lives on the headless/direct-CDP path (T11); the extension/sidepanel path is explicitly non-stealth and we ship no 'undetectable' claims.

options.label: Stealth = headless/direct-CDP path only; extension is non-stealth
Stealth in the real browser is a hard requirement
Drop T12 (stealth) entirely from this program

questions.prompt: How aggressive should T10 (profile / cookies) be?
questions.description: Reading the user's real Chrome cookie store (SQLite) for selective per-site import is the daily-driver realism win, but it's root-equivalent over web identity and needs explicit consent. Default profile stays isolated either way.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Selective per-site cookie import into a dedicated non-primary profile, behind an explicit per-site consent prompt. Isolated profile remains the default; full real-profile use stays the opt-in --use-default-profile path.

options.label: Selective per-site cookie import with explicit consent
Just --use-default-profile; no cookie extraction
Both, fully user-configurable

questions.prompt: Which guardrails must hold across the whole program? (select all that apply)
questions.description: Invariants the program must not break — used to bound scope and write 'must still be true' facts.
questions.recommendedAnswer: All four: existing single-session sidepanel UX keeps working; bridge CLI surface stays additive (no breaking changes); AGPL + local-first IndexedDB storage preserved; no 'undetectable' / bypass marketing claims.

options.label: Existing single-session sidepanel UX keeps working
Bridge CLI surface stays additive (no breaking changes)
Preserve AGPL + local-first IndexedDB storage
No 'undetectable' / bypass marketing claims
